steps.title: Identify the Invention Timeline
steps.content:
The passage states that swords were first developed during the Middle Bronze Age (14th–10th century BCE).

The Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard is from the 16th century BCE (before this) and the Horsehope Craig hoard is from the 7th century BCE (after).

This chronological gap determines which hoard should contain evidence of sword production.

steps.title: Apply the Timeline Signal to the Choices
steps.content:
Choice D correctly states that sword evidence appears in the later Horsehope Craig hoard but not in the earlier Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard—the exact contrast explained by when swords were first developed.

The other choices either claim both hoards contain evidence, reverse this relationship, or base their reasoning on discovery dates rather than artifact dates.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
